title: Malaxis cuprea
uses: Malaxis cuprea, also known as the copper orchid, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes. In some cultures, the plant is believed to possess antimicrobial properties and is used to treat wounds and skin infections. The leaves of Malaxis cuprea are also said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as fever and rheumatism. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Malaxis cuprea for medicinal use. Despite its potential benefits, the plant is not widely recognized or studied in modern medicine.
taxonomy: Malaxis cuprea, also known as the copper orchid, is classified within the family Orchidaceae. This orchid is further classified within the genus Malaxis. Malaxis cuprea is native to South America and has several subspecies. It belongs to the subtribe Malaxidinae, which is a part of the tribe Cranichideae. The species is also known by other names, such as Malaxis coccinea and Cranichis coccinea.
